perturbation.original_text: 5. Custom er and Minnesota Power hereby acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Inform ation m ay result in irreparable harm to Minnesota Power. Accordingly, Custom er agrees that Minnesota Power shall ha ve the right to obtain a tem porary restraining order enjoining disclosure or use of all Inform ation subject to this Agreem ent. Custom er agrees not to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order. Such order shall becom e perm anent upon adequate showing of irreparable inju ry and ruling by a court of law. The Parties may participate in any contest relating to a perm anent injunction.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Custom er and Minnesota Power hereby acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Inform ation m ay result in harm to Minnesota Power. Accordingly, Custom er agrees that Minnesota Power shall ha ve the right to seek mediation before obtaining a tem porary restraining order enjoining disclosure or use of all Inform ation subject to this Agreem ent. Custom er reserves the right to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order. Such order shall becom e perm anent upon adequate showing of inju ry and ruling by a court of law. The Parties may participate in any contest relating to a perm anent injunction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the customer agrees not to contest a temporary restraining order, while the modified text says the customer reserves the right to contest it and that harm would not be assumed to be irreparable. This directly contradicts the original intent of swiftly preventing disclosure and creates an in-text contradiction in the section 5 itself. The phrase 'Custom er agrees not to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order' is now the exact opposite.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Custom er and Minnesota Power hereby acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Inform ation m ay result in harm to Minnesota Power. Accordingly, Custom er agrees that Minnesota Power shall ha ve the right to seek mediation before obtaining a tem porary restraining order enjoining disclosure or use of all Inform ation subject to this Agreem ent. Custom er reserves the right to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order. Such order shall becom e perm anent upon adequate showing of inju ry and ruling by a court of law. The Parties may participate in any contest relating to a perm anent injunction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself within section 5. The original text stated that the customer agrees not to contest a temporary restraining order. The changed text states 'Custom er reserves the right to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order'. This creates a direct conflict within the same section, rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The explanation provided further supports this by stating the phrase 'Custom er agrees not to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order' is now the exact opposite.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Upon the written request of Minnesota Power, the Custom er shall return to Minnesota Power all docum entation and electr onic m edia containing Inform ation and shall provide written certif ication that copies of all Inf ormation have been destroyed or returned.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Upon the written request of Minnesota Power, the Custom er shall return to Minnesota Power all docum entation and electr onic m edia containing Inform ation if it is reasonably possible and shall attempt to provide written certif ication that copies of all Inf ormation have been destroyed or returned.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the customer *shall* return and provide certification, making it an obligation. The modified text introduces ambiguity by saying 'if it is reasonably possible' and 'shall attempt to provide', weakening the obligation. This creates an in-text contradiction if any other section relies on the Customer's absolute obligation to return and certify destruction of the information, such as section 5 and 9. It weakens the ability of Minnesota Power to reclaim the information as specified in the rest of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Custom er and Minnesota Power hereby acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Inform ation m ay result in harm to Minnesota Power. Accordingly, Custom er agrees that Minnesota Power shall ha ve the right to seek mediation before obtaining a tem porary restraining order enjoining disclosure or use of all Inform ation subject to this Agreem ent. Custom er reserves the right to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order. Such order shall becom e perm anent upon adequate showing of inju ry and ruling by a court of law. The Parties may participate in any contest relating to a perm anent injunction.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6 mandates the customer to return all documentation and provide written certification. The changed text weakens this obligation by introducing 'if it is reasonably possible' and 'shall attempt to provide', creating ambiguity. This directly contradicts Section 5, which assumes the customer's absolute obligation regarding the information. Section 5 states that the Customer acknowledges potential harm to Minnesota Power from unauthorized disclosure and agrees Minnesota Power has the right to seek injunctive relief. By weakening the obligation to return and certify destruction, the changed text undermines Minnesota Power's ability to prevent unauthorized disclosure, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. In further consideration of Minnesota Power disclosing its Information hereunder, Customer agrees that it will not use the Informa tion for any third-party’s benefit or in providing services to third parties (which shall specifically include any wholesale or retail customer served by Minnesota Power).
perturbation.changed_text: 8. In further consideration of Minnesota Power disclosing its Information hereunder, Customer agrees that it will not use the Informa tion for any third-party’s direct benefit that directly competes with Minnesota Power or in providing services that directly compete with Minnesota Power.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the word 'direct' and 'that directly competes', the scope of the restriction on the use of Information is narrowed. This introduces ambiguity and creates an in-text contradiction, if the other sections and the overarching aim of the document expect the Customer to use the information only for Minnesota Power interconnection and for no other purpose, especially section 3 and 7. Now the Customer can use the information to do things for 3rd parties as long as the benefit/service isn't directly competing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. It is agreed that the Inf ormation will be used by Custom er only f or the lim ited purpose of interconnecting a distributed generation system with Minnesota Power. The Customer may disclose the Information to anyone as long as they believe it will help with the interconnection project. This Ag reem ent shall not prohibit disclosure to third party consultants and professional advisors provided such third parties enter into and agree to be bound by this Agreem ent.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8 prevented the Customer from using Minnesota Power's Information for any third party's benefit. The changed text in Section 8 narrows this restriction to only apply to third parties that directly compete with Minnesota Power. Section 3 states that the Information will be used only for interconnecting a distributed generation system. Now the Customer can use the information for 3rd parties as long as the benefit/service isn't directly competing, which contradicts the requirement in Section 3 that it is only used for interconnection.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement shall be effective for a term of ______ (__) years from the day first written above. Notwithstanding the Parties entering into any further agreement or arrangement, or any other event or occurrence, the provisions of this Agreement relating to nondisclosure (including the provisions relating to the breach of this Agreement) or use of the Information shall remain in full force and eff ect for ________ years or as long as the Information is confidential and non-public, whichever is less, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement shall be effective for a term of ______ (__) years from the day first written above. Notwithstanding the Parties entering into any further agreement or arrangement, or any other event or occurrence, the provisions of this Agreement relating to nondisclosure (including the provisions relating to the breach of this Agreement) or use of the Information shall remain in full force and eff ect for ________ years or as long as the Information is confidential and non-public, whichever is less, unless otherwise determined by a court of law.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties hereto' to 'otherwise determined by a court of law', the power to modify the non-disclosure terms shifts from the parties to a court. This contradicts the overall spirit of the contract, which is a mutual agreement between Minnesota Power and the Customer. It also puts the other sections in question in terms of which sections the court can make changes to, particularly sections that stipulate that they can't be contested by the customer, such as section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Custom er and Minnesota Power hereby acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Inform ation m ay result in harm to Minnesota Power. Accordingly, Custom er agrees that Minnesota Power shall ha ve the right to seek mediation before obtaining a tem porary restraining order enjoining disclosure or use of all Inform ation subject to this Agreem ent. Custom er reserves the right to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order. Such order shall becom e perm anent upon adequate showing of inju ry and ruling by a court of law. The Parties may participate in any contest relating to a perm anent injunction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 9, altering who has the power to modify the non-disclosure terms from the parties themselves to a court of law. This directly contradicts section 5, which grants the Customer the right to contest a temporary restraining order but implies that the overall agreement is between the parties. The change introduces a third party (the court) that can alter the terms agreed upon by the Customer and Minnesota Power, thus contradicting the mutual agreement outlined in section 5. Section 5 highlights the right to contest a temporary restraining order, stemming from the agreement, whereas the changed text could imply a court of law has jurisdiction over the entire agreement, creating a direct conflict.
